EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong mid-level jet moved into the Rocky Mountain Front. This combined with a strong surface pressure gradient and mountain wave activity brought isolated strong winds to the Rocky Mountain Front.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Light to moderate snow occurred as relatively warm and moist air spread over cold valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow covered roads brought moderate travel impacts with 1 to 3 inches of snow common for lower elevations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Light to moderate snow occurred as relatively warm and moist air spread over cold valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Slick roads occurred with lane blockage for a time near Lookout Pass on Interstate 90 as 1 to 3 inches of snow fell.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Light to moderate snow occurred as relatively warm and moist air spread over cold valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A light wintry mix brought very slippery conditions to area roadways. Snow amounts were generally less than an inch with up to a tenth of ice accumulation from freezing rain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Light to moderate snow occurred as relatively warm and moist air spread over cold valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A light wintry mix brought changeable and slippery roads. A crash blocked a lane on Interstate 90 for a time west of Drummond.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Light to moderate snow occurred as relatively warm and moist air spread over cold valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A light wintry mix resulted in slick road conditions across the area. On Homestake Pass, chain requirements were put into effect due to the conditions. A vehicle crash associated with the weather blocked both westbound lanes of Interstate 90 just west of the Pass.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An atmospheric river brought heavy snow to pass level locations and moderate snow to valleys of northwest Montana.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy, wet snowfall of 3 to 5 inches fell in the valleys, resulting in localized power outages near Heron. Icy roads led to at least one car accident and numerous reports of slick conditions.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An atmospheric river brought heavy snow to pass level locations and moderate snow to valleys of northwest Montana.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lookout Ski reported heavy snow with 16 inches of new accumulation.  Dispatch reported that semi-trucks blocked Interstate 90 at Lookout Pass, where 7 to 8 vehicles spun out and some travelers remained stuck overnight. In fact, between 4:00 AM and 5:00 AM on the 7th, the Mullan Pass ASOS estimated intense snowfall rates of 2 to 3 inches per hour. Additionally, the Heron 2 NW COOP station reported 6.7 inches of new snow, and power outages were noted in Haugan for a period of time. An additional 2 inches of new snow fell at the Lookout SNOTEL site on the 8th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An atmospheric river brought heavy snow to pass level locations and moderate snow to valleys of northwest Montana.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lolo Pass SNOTEL reported 13 inches of new snow with webcams showing snow covered roadways.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An atmospheric river brought heavy snow to pass level locations and moderate snow to valleys of northwest Montana.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: SNOTEL reports showed 5 to 12 inches of new snow, while webcams and road reports indicated snow-covered roadways. These conditions led to chain requirements near Marias Pass on the morning of the 7th, where a vehicle crash resulted in blocked lanes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Stratus overspread the Flathead Valley as moisture moved northward from Flathead Lake. Temperatures in the 20s and a light drizzle resulted in icy conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Social media reports indicated widespread black ice resulting in hazardous driving conditions across the area. Multiple vehicle accidents and slide-offs occurred on Highways 93 and 35, as well as at local intersections including KM Ranch Road. At least one vehicle was totaled.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Light to moderate snow occurred as relatively warm and moist air spread over cold valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread 1 to 2 inch snow amounts brought slick roads to many valley locations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Light to moderate snow occurred as relatively warm and moist air spread over cold valleys.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Light to moderate snow fell across the region, with the heaviest accumulations of 2 to 3 inches occurring across the Flathead Valley. These totals resulted in slick road conditions for much of the area. Further south, the Mission Valley saw only minor accumulations, with totals generally remaining under 1 inch.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific weather system moved across the region during the morning of 1/7. An associated cold front brought scattered to widespread snow showers to Southwest MT and supported the development of a snow squall along I-90 near Homestake Pass. This cold front also brought isolated gusty winds to Southwest MT.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: ASOS station KDLN Dillon Airport measured a 59 mph wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific weather system moved across the region during the morning of 1/7. An associated cold front brought scattered to widespread snow showers to Southwest MT and supported the development of a snow squall along I-90 near Homestake Pass. This cold front also brought isolated gusty winds to Southwest MT.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow Squall. Homestake Pass DOT camera on I-90 showed a rapid reduction in visibility, to near-whiteout, with light snow accumulations on the highway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper-level disturbance moving through the region brought strong winds aloft and strengthened the surface pressure gradient along the Rocky Mountain Front. This brought a period of high winds to the Rocky Mountain Front on the 11th. These stronger winds aloft progressed east across the plains on the 12th, which brought high winds to North-Central MT, primarily along the Montana Highway 200 Corridor.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mesonet station SNPMT Sunpoint measured a high wind gust. Mesonet station LGPVC Logan Pass (GNP) and Mesonet station SRYM8 Saint Mary RAWS also had a high wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper-air disturbance helped to drive an Arctic airmass from Canada into Northern MT. Winds were generally light, but north winds helped lock the cold airmass into the Hi-line on the morning of the 23rd. Extreme cold conditions developed as a result.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mesonet station GW4015 Chester Four NE reported a -38 wind chill.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Jordan Airport ASOS (KJDN) measured a 61 mph wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An arctic air mass combined with moderate or gusty winds to bring extreme cold conditions to portions of northeast Montana near the Canadian border.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind chill values of -50 or worse were common in Sheridan County. The lowest wind chill value of -55 was recorded at the NDAWN station Dooley 3S.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: This episode of freezing fog mainly affected areas near and north of US-Highway 2.  Main impacts were near zero visibility and rime icing on power lines, which lead to some scattered power outages.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power lines in the area were weighed down and stretched causing brief power outages across the area. Near zero visibility created hazardous driving conditions at times.||Less than a quarter mile visibility was not consistently reported at available stations (i.e. Wolf Point Airport ASOS and Poplar AWOS) during this period.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds spread across the mountains and into the lower elevations of south-central Montana. Gusts 58 to 75 mph occurred with this event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Judith Gap DOT station gusted to 58 mph.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power was out for more than 24 hours at a residence in Larslan. Power lines in the area were weighed down and stretched causing brief power outages across the area. Near zero visibility created hazardous driving conditions at times.||Less than a quarter mile visibility was not consistently reported at available stations (i.e. Glasgow Airport ASOS) during this period.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system from the Canadian Rockies dropped south across northeast Montana, producing strong damaging winds. Some snow showers accompanied the system, dropping visibility to a mile (Poplar and Glasgow).
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The BLM Zortman E mesonet site (MTM38) measured a 64 mph wind gust.
